channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Embark Studios Ousts Co-Founder Following Sexual Misconduct Allegations
item.content:encoded:
Embark Studios Co-Founder and CCO Rob Runesson is out at the company following allegations of sexual misconduct.

First reported by IGN’s Rebekah Valentine, Runesson’s departure comes via what Embark is calling a “mutual” agreement to part ways.

Allegations against Runesson came in March, claiming that he engaged in sexual misconduct against a “popular female streamer” of Embark’s game The Finals. The accuser claims that the streamer became “increasingly uncomfortable” with Runesson’s actions, but believed they couldn’t push back against it due to their need for promotion of her streams in order to earn income.

In a statement to IGN, Embark confirmed the departure after being made aware of the allegations and hired a firm to investigate the claims.

“Our leadership team was recently made aware of allegations against an Embark employee,” the studio said. “We take these matters seriously, and as a part of our process for addressing any types of allegations, we immediately hired a law firm to conduct an external investigation. The investigation did not substantiate the allegations, but we found the situation unsustainable and mutually agreed to part ways with Rob.”

IGN reached out to the streamer, who chose not to comment on the record. However, they claim that “a number of the allegations circulated were out of context or misleading.”

What do you think of Runesson’s departure from Embark Studios? Leave your thoughts in the comments below, and in this official Insider Gaming Discord.

For more news, read about IOI Partners officially ending its partnership with Build a Rocket Boy. And for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our weekly newsletter.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

item.title: Logitech G Unveils RS H-Shifter Gear Shift
item.content:encoded:
Logitech G is adding to its racing offering for sim drivers. The company has announced its brand new Logitech G RS H-Shifter, a manual 7+R shifter that pairs with the RS 50 wheelbase.

“For years, racers have yearned for a product that combines modern engineering with the timeless precision of manual control, and Logitech G has delivered,” Logitech G said via release. “The RS H-Shifter offers seamless cross-platform compatibility and customization, making it an essential tool for both seasoned veterans and new enthusiasts yearning for the visceral thrill of manual control.” 

The RS H-Shifter features a custom 7-speed shift mechanism with reserve, along with a push-through lockout that prevents an accidental shift to 7th or reverse. There’s also a Hall Effect sensor that is designed to “ensure long-term reliability, addressing the reliability issues in the old G-series shifter design.”

Connection-wise, the shifter is built to connect directly to the PRO/RS50 wheel base for use on console or directly to your PC via USB 2.0. It can even be used with other G-Series wheels via the Racing Adapter.

Lastly, the shifter allows for customization with aftermarket pieces. Whether you purchase one or 3D print your own, you can swap out the shift knob or shaft extender to optimize your personal setup.

The Logitech G RS H-Shifter is available now for $159.99/€159.99 via Logitech G’s website.

Are you going to pick up an RS H-Shifter for your setup? Leave your thoughts in the comments below, and in this official Insider Gaming Discord.

And for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our weekly newsletter.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

item.title: Death Stranding 2 Devs Reveal New PC and PS5 Features
item.content:encoded:
As Death Stranding 2’s PC release draws closer, new features, game modes, and more have been revealed for both platforms.

Death Stranding 2: On the Beach’s PC release date was revealed during PlayStation’s last State of Play event, with Kojima Productions’ latest title arriving for a new platform this week. We were informed that new modes and features will be introduced for both platforms, and now we finally know what they are.

Death Stranding 2 PC Comes With ‘To the Wilder Mode’, New Training, Chriral Feline and More

Heading into the new features and modes for Death Stranding 2 on PC and PS5, the first mode to highlight is ‘To the Wilder.’ This new difficulty will push players’ porter skills to the limit in unforgiving environments filled with dangerous enemies. Once players embark on this quest, there’s no turning back. However, the rewards for completing it are significantly greater.

Sam will be able to gain new fieldware, like new bandanas, through a new VR training area called “Trapped in a Strange Realm”, which can be used to replay the fight with Neil. Players will also see the Chiral Feline visiting their private room in the DHV Magellan, and it will also be accessible through the photo mode.

Furthermore, Death Stranding 2 PC players will also have access to features like Ray Tracing on Reflections and Ambient Occlusion if they have a high-end PC. The PC version provides full integration with Nvidia’s DLSS, AMD’s FSR, and Intel’s XeSS, as well as upscaling and frame generation technologies, uncapped frame rates, and customised graphical settings. Additionally, if you want the PS5 experience, there is full DualSense controller integration with haptic feedback, adaptive triggers, and Spatial Sound Support.

Interested readers can check out the PlayStation blog post for more details.

In other news, here are Death Stranding 2’s PC system requirements. Additionally, check out whether Kojima Productions has added Denuvo DRM to the title. What are your thoughts on the new modes and features for Death Stranding 2 PC and PS5? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Take-Two CEO stating that AI being able to generate hit games is ‘laughable’. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Take-Two CEO Says AI Generating A Hit Game Is “Laughable”
item.content:encoded:
Take-Two CEO recently spoke about AI tools, stating that they can help create assets, but not provide hits for developers.

AI use in the gaming industry has been a subject of contention ever since the technology was revealed. Most recently, we saw Nvidia’s DLSS 5 being slammed by players, with some calling it “a cheap AI-driven image filter.” Now, the CEO of the publisher of one of the biggest games out there has shared his two cents.

Take-Two CEO Says AI Can Help Create Assets But Not Generate Hits

In a recent interview with The Game Business, Take-Two CEO Strauss Zelnick discussed the use and capabilities of AI tools in the gaming industry. Zelnick emphasized that while it’s clear these tools offer significant benefits, they don’t guarantee that “everyone can create hits.” He acknowledged that while AI can assist developers in creating assets, it does not necessarily lead to the creation of successful games.

Zelnick explains that it doesn’t matter whether developers created an asset by simply pushing a button or spent six weeks crafting it; in the end, it’s still an asset. However, he also notes that while a developer can create assets that may resemble a major release, such as NBA 2K or EA Sports FC, “creating a hit of that magnitude is a completely different animal and does require human engagement and creativity.”

When asked if a project like Project Genie could simplify the process for developers creating games like GTA 6, Take-Two’s CEO, Strauss Zelnick, responded, “not even the littlest bit.” He explained that there is already a significant amount of technology available to assist developers in making video games, resulting in thousands of games being released each year. However, successful titles tend to come from “large entertainment companies” or, occasionally, indie studios, which are generally well-funded and quite capable on their own.

The Take-Two CEO added, “The notion that somehow new tools would allow an individual to push a button and generate a hit and bring it to many millions of consumers around the world, it’s a laughable notion.” Zelnick elaborated that this has never been the case in the entertainment industry. He provided an example of music created in response to specific prompts. According to Zelnick, these types of tracks cannot be heard more than once; they serve a purpose similar to that of a birthday greeting card for your partner.

In other news, a GTA 6 hacker has suggested the game’s source code could already be in the hands of someone. Additionally, the CEO of Take-Two Interactive has confirmed that no generative AI is used in Grand Theft Auto 6. What do you think about Take-Two CEO’s thoughts on the use of AI tools in the gaming industry? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about IO Interactive ending its publishing partnership with Build a Rocket Boy. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.
